# W20T4 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Implement the mutable indexing surface so element mutation is available only when the storage representation satisfies `StorageMut`.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/17-indexing.md` §5.2
- `design/17-indexing.md` §6.4
- `design/17-indexing.md` §8.2
- `design/17-indexing.md` §10
- `design/00-coding.md` §2.3, §2.5

## Dependencies

- W20T2

## Files to Create/Modify

- `src/index/access.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Public method signatures

Implement the mutable access surface exactly:

```rust
impl<S, D, A> TensorBase<S, D>
where
    S: StorageMut<Elem = A>,
    D: Dimension,
{
    pub fn try_at_mut<I>(&mut self, index: I) -> Result<&mut A, XenonError>
    where
        I: NdIndex<D>;

    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, index: &[usize]) -> Result<&mut A, XenonError>;

    pub unsafe fn get_unchecked_mut(&mut self, index: &[usize]) -> &mut A;
}
```

### Trait bound boundary

- These methods must exist only when `S: StorageMut<Elem = A>`.
- Do not add a runtime `InvalidStorageMode` fallback.
- Read only storage types simply must not expose the mutable methods.

### Semantic requirements

- Checked mutable methods use the same rank and bounds validation as the read only methods.
- `get_unchecked_mut` skips validation only.
- Mutable valid checked and unchecked paths must refer to the same logical element.
- The caller must hold exclusive mutable access to the target element.

## Test Plan

- `test_try_at_mut_requires_storage_mut`
- mutable success path test on owned storage
- mutable bounds failure test returning `IndexOutOfBounds`
- checked versus unchecked mutable parity test

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `try_at_mut`, `get_mut`, and `get_unchecked_mut` exist with the exact signatures above
- [ ] The methods are gated purely by `StorageMut`
- [ ] Checked mutable errors mirror the read only checked error model
- [ ] No runtime storage mode error is added for read only representations

## Development Workflow

1. Write compile and runtime tests for the `StorageMut` boundary.
2. Reuse the checked offset logic from the read only path.
3. Implement mutable safe methods.
4. Implement the unchecked mutable method.
5. Verify parity with the read only behavior on valid indices.
